issues.objection: A cynical writer's mocking gloss of 7.24 would target either (a) 'just commit to your stack, bro' or (b) 'work harder, but spiritual.' The engineering layer forecloses both: the falsifiability field explicitly rejects the soft reading; the counter_example carves out where the verse does not apply. The quotable_line ("The architecture diagram is real but partial; the failure-mode tree the senior carries in her head is the substrate the …") is concrete enough to resist parody. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Verse claims metaphysical concealment (avyaktaṁ vyaktim āpannaṁ mistaken). Engineering analog scoped narrowly: architecture diagrams are partial; the substrate carried by the senior is not directly diagrammable. Counter-example: stale tacit assumption is not the verse's paraṁ bhāvam.

notes: Cynical-writer test for 7.24: identify the parody-handle. The verse does not give one — the engineering layer is anchored to specific tooling and configurations; the quotable line is testable on the artifact, not on affect. PASS.
